# Decision Pack

<!-- Framework default template. The ## headings below are the required
     sections the required-sections sensor checks. A team override at
     aidlc/spaces/<space>/memory/templates/decision-pack.md wins. -->

## Where this came from

Who asked for this and how often, carried forward from the intent-statement,
with anything the open-questions-record later settled or reopened about the
ask.

## The working artifact

A runnable mock-up, a visual walkthrough, a data readout, or a working slice
of the path the future-state proposes — demonstrable things communicate in a
way documents do not.

## Narrative

The case in the organization's own proposal format, asked for once when
this pack is first drafted — defaulting to a plain one-page summary of
problem, evidence, recommendation, and cost where no format exists.

## Evidence summary

Every assumption tested, its outcome and its source — summarized from the
assumptions-record and the evidence-record, never re-derived — the open
risks, what was set aside along the way, and the cost spent, where cost
reporting is on.

## Appetite

How much the organization is willing to invest in this direction — a
boundary, not an estimate.

## No-gos

What this initiative will not do.

## Decision

Recorded when the person answers the decision question in this stage; the
exit gate ratifies it. Commit, pivot, or park — who decided, and the date.
For park, add the sponsor narrative: what we learned, why not now, and what
would reopen it.
